headline: Syariah compliance officers monitor 4,200 businesses
context: State religious authorities deployed syariah compliance officers to monitor 4,200 food and entertainment businesses in 2024, raising civil liberties questions.

cards.big: 4,200 businesses monitored by syariah compliance officers across 9 states in 2024
cards.sub: Entertainment venues and food establishments subject to unannounced inspections

cards.big: State syariah enactments grant enforcement officers powers of entry and search
cards.sub: Warrantless inspections of premises raise questions about Article 13 property rights

cards.big: Non-Muslim business owners in 340 cases reported being subject to syariah inspections
cards.sub: Jurisdictional boundaries between civil and religious enforcement remain unclear

cards.big: Entertainment industry operators reported 23% revenue decline due to compliance costs
cards.sub: Businesses relocate or close rather than face repeated inspections

cards.big: Monitoring 4,200 businesses for religious compliance blurs state and faith boundaries
Malaysian Indian Congress raised jurisdictional concerns in Parliament in 2024
